title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 1
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: A university careers service has asked students to submit a report on the most effective ways to prepare for job interviews. Your report should identify the key factors that contribute to a successful interview performance, describe common mistakes candidates make, and recommend how the careers service could better support students.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Effective Interview Preparation for University Students

Introduction
This report was prepared at the request of the University Careers Service and draws on a survey of forty final-year students who attended mock interview sessions during the autumn term. It identifies the key determinants of strong interview performance, highlights frequent errors, and makes recommendations for improving the support offered to students.

Key Factors in Successful Interviews
Feedback from professional assessors consistently identified three qualities in candidates who performed well: thorough knowledge of the prospective employer's business and sector; the ability to articulate their own skills and experiences with specific, well-chosen examples; and confident, measured communication—particularly the capacity to structure answers clearly under pressure.

Common Mistakes
The most frequently observed errors were:
- Providing vague or overly general answers that failed to demonstrate personal contribution or measurable impact.
- Insufficient knowledge of the company's recent news, products, or values.
- Poor management of nervous body language, including excessive fidgeting and avoidance of eye contact.

Recommendations
The Careers Service should consider the following enhancements to its support provision:
- Expanding the mock interview programme to include sector-specific sessions with external recruiters.
- Creating a digital repository of recorded model answers to common competency questions, categorised by industry.
- Offering short workshops on professional body language and techniques for managing interview anxiety.

Conclusion
Interview performance is a learnable skill. With targeted practice and expert guidance, the Careers Service can significantly improve student outcomes in this critical area of professional development.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt: You have been a loyal customer of a local independent bookshop for many years, but you have recently learned that it is at risk of closing due to competition from online retailers. Write a letter to the owner expressing your support, explaining what the shop means to you and the community, and suggesting practical ways in which the shop might attract more customers.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Mr. Fennell,

I was deeply saddened to hear of the difficulties that Pages & Co. is currently facing, and I felt compelled to write to express my wholehearted support and to share some thoughts that I hope might be of some small use.

I have been visiting your shop for nearly a decade, and it occupies a place in my life that no website could ever replicate. The experience of browsing your carefully curated shelves, discovering books I would never have thought to search for, and receiving your personal recommendations has enriched my reading life immeasurably. Beyond my own experience, I know from conversations with neighbours and colleagues that Pages & Co. is regarded by many as one of the genuine assets of this neighbourhood—a cultural anchor in a high street that has otherwise become increasingly generic.

I appreciate that competing with the pricing and convenience of large online retailers is an enormous challenge. However, I believe there are avenues that could help strengthen your customer base and build new revenue streams. A regular programme of author events and reading groups would draw readers to the shop as a community space rather than merely a retail outlet. A 'book subscription' service, curated personally by you for different reader profiles, might appeal strongly to customers who value your expertise. Partnerships with local schools for author visits or curriculum book supply could also bring in reliable institutional income.

I sincerely hope that Pages & Co. finds a path forward. This community needs it.

Yours sincerely,
Helen Marchetti

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: An international online magazine is collecting reviews of theatre or live performance experiences that readers found genuinely extraordinary. Describe the performance, what made it memorable, and whether you would recommend it. Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Theatre That Stays With You: 'The Inheritance' at the Noël Coward Theatre

Some theatrical experiences entertain you for an evening. A very few change something in you permanently. Matthew Lopez's 'The Inheritance', which I had the extraordinary fortune to see during its West End run, belongs firmly in the latter category.

The production, which runs across two evenings totalling approximately six hours, traces the lives of a group of gay men in New York across several decades, weaving together themes of love, loss, AIDS, memory, and what it means to inherit a history. The scale of the ambition is matched by the execution: the writing is simultaneously expansive and intimate, moving without sentimentality, and unfailingly intelligent.

What elevates 'The Inheritance' above almost any other contemporary play I have seen is its relationship with time. The narrative moves freely between decades and registers, using the device of E.M. Forster as a kind of narrative spirit guide. Yet despite its structural complexity, the emotional logic is always pellucid; by the end of the second evening, characters who began as strangers feel as intimately known as old friends. The final scene, in particular, achieves a quality of catharsis that is genuinely rare in the theatre.

The production demands a significant investment of time and emotional energy. It repays both with extraordinary generosity. For anyone with the opportunity to see it in future productions or revivals, I cannot recommend it strongly enough. It is, without qualification, one of the greatest pieces of theatre I have encountered.
